content.problemStatement:
You are given an odd integer NNN and two integer sequences A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ and B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.

Your task is to reorder the elements of BBB, forming a new sequence C1,C2,…,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1​,C2​,…,CN​ (i.e. choose a permutation P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​ of the integers 111 through NNN, where Ci=BPiC_i = B_{P_i}Ci​=BPi​​ for each valid iii), in such a way that the following condition holds: (A1⊕C1)=(A2⊕C2)=…=(AN⊕CN)(A_1 \oplus C_1) = (A_2 \oplus C_2) = \ldots = (A_N \oplus C_N)(A1​⊕C1​)=(A2​⊕C2​)=…=(AN​⊕CN​), where ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes bitwise XOR. Find one such reordered sequence or determine that it is impossible.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If there is no valid way to reorder the sequence BBB, print a single line containing the integer −1-1−1.
Otherwise, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,…,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1​,C2​,…,CN​. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one.
